paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree
summary: Question 4.1 from arXiv:2503.20045 asks for which fixed digraphs H there exists a constant c_ε ≥ 1 for all ε > 0 such that every digraph D with χ(D) ≥ c_ε and minimum out-degree at least ε|V(D)| contains H as a subdigraph; this generalises the paper's main result (Theorem 1.3), which settles the question for all cycle orientations. The paper itself notes two families with affirmative answers: the cycle orientations characterised by Theorem 1.3, and subdigraphs of directed complete bipartite digraphs. No follow-up paper addressing the general question was found.

notes: The source paper was published in Combinatorica 46, 12 (2026) (doi:10.1007/s00493-026-00207-0). Question 4.1 is a broad open problem posed in the paper; the characterisation for cycle orientations (Theorem 1.3) and the Ramsey-type argument for subdigraphs of directed complete bipartite digraphs are the only known partial answers. No follow-up resolving the general question was found in the indexed literature as of 2026-05-14.
